description: stopRecord metadata outputs: after a recording saves, the stopRecord step exposes recordingPath/format/duration/width/height/fps in outputs, capturable via step variables. One test per output-bearing format family: mp4 (fps token in ffmpeg stderr) and gif (fps derived from the tbr fallback). Headed-only like all real recordings; the skip path (no outputs on a skipped stopRecord) is asserted programmatically in test/core-core.test.js.

tests.description: An mp4 recording reports probe-derived metadata outputs.

steps.description: Assert the captured outputs: format matches, path non-empty, and every probed metric is a positive number.
steps.runShell: node -e "const ok = process.env.REC_MP4_FORMAT === 'mp4' && require('fs').existsSync(process.env.REC_MP4_PATH) && Number(process.env.REC_MP4_DURATION) > 0 && Number(process.env.REC_MP4_WIDTH) > 0 && Number(process.env.REC_MP4_HEIGHT) > 0 && Number(process.env.REC_MP4_FPS) > 0; process.exit(ok ? 0 : 1)"

steps.description: Assert the guards didn't just pass but measured: resolutionMatch and allBlack must be the real verdicts, not unresolved variables. A guard that silently never ran would leave the step green while proving nothing.
steps.runShell: node -e "const e={res:process.env.REC_MP4_RES_MATCH,black:process.env.REC_MP4_ALL_BLACK};const ok = e.res === 'true' && e.black === 'false'; if (!ok) { console.error('verify guards did not report:', JSON.stringify(e)); process.exit(1) }"

tests.description: A gif recording reports metadata outputs, exercising the tbr fps fallback (gif streams carry no fps token in ffmpeg stderr).

steps.description: Assert the gif outputs: format matches and duration/fps are positive (fps comes from the tbr fallback).
steps.runShell: node -e "const ok = process.env.REC_GIF_FORMAT === 'gif' && Number(process.env.REC_GIF_DURATION) > 0 && Number(process.env.REC_GIF_FPS) > 0; process.exit(ok ? 0 : 1)"

tests.description: ADR 01080: the `false` shape of each guard is a no-op, not an error. `resolution: false` and `notBlack: false` must skip their checks — reporting no verdict rather than a default one — while a sibling duration guard still runs. (Omitting `verify` entirely is the other disabling form; the gif test above covers it.)

steps.description: The duration guard ran (duration is a positive number and the step passed), while the disabled guards reported nothing at all — their outputs stay unresolved rather than reporting a verdict the run never made.
steps.runShell: node -e "const unset = (v) => v !== 'true' && v !== 'false';const e={dur:process.env.REC_OFF_DURATION,res:process.env.REC_OFF_RES_MATCH,black:process.env.REC_OFF_ALL_BLACK};const ok = Number(e.dur) > 0 && unset(e.res) && unset(e.black); if (!ok) { console.error('disabled guards should report no verdict:', JSON.stringify(e)); process.exit(1) }"
